title: Side Hutches (Board and Batten Collection)
summary: A tall open-shelf hutch with 2x2 board-and-batten legs framing three shelves — a plain shelving piece, unlike this catalog's board-and-batten pieces that carry drawers or doors.
description:
Two full-height 1x12 sides carry three shelves between them, with 2x2 legs running the front and back and 1x2 side trim finishing the visible edges — this is the same board-and-batten leg-and-trim system as this catalog's other pieces in the collection, but here it's applied to a completely open shelving hutch with no drawers or doors at all.

The three shelves get their own 2x2 and 1x2 shelf trim, matching the leg treatment so the whole piece reads as one consistent design. **The plywood back is optional** — the hutch is fully usable without it, so add it only if a finished look from behind matters for where it's placed.

This piece pairs as a side hutch alongside this collection's console and dresser pieces, but builds and stands entirely on its own.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two sides at 48" and three shelves at 15-1/2" from the 1x12. Cut four side trim at 11-1/2" and two shelf trim, narrow at 15-1/2" from the 1x2. Cut four legs at 48" and five shelf trim, wide at 15-1/2" from the 2x2. Cut one back at 48" from the 1/4" plywood, 48 in x 18-1/2 in (optional back). Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to two 8 ft 1x12, one 8 ft 1x2 and four 8 ft 2x2. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Build the sides and legs
steps.body: Attach the side trim and legs to the two side panels.

steps.title: Add the shelves
steps.body: Attach the shelf trim to each shelf, then mount the three shelves between the sides at even spacing.

steps.title: Add the optional back
steps.body: Attach the plywood back panel if a finished rear view is wanted.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply finishing supplies.

unresolvedImages.alt: Side hutch with open shelves in the board and batten collection
